claim_boundary: Only the current route decision, executable model boundary, and consumed current evidence are claimed; no future behavior or unrun route is certified.

closure_rules.scope: current target revision and declared route only

forbidden_shortcuts.summary: Do not create a SkillGuard-owned alternate execution route.
Do not convert configured booleans, generated JSON, or progress into native pass evidence.
Do not skip required stages, checks, receipts, or blockers.

integration_claim_boundary: SkillGuard gates the target-native FlowGuard route; it does not execute a parallel controller or replace native model/test evidence.

native_check_bindings.evidence_source: current model runner receipt and final report

native_check_inventory.summary: Run the minimum valuable model-first entry model.

native_route_bindings.source: FlowGuard canonical route registry and target contract source

not_parallel_route_proof.evidence_source: Canonical route registry, contract source, and may_define_* false gates
not_parallel_route_proof.summary: SkillGuard only validates the FlowGuard-owned route and cannot define an alternate successful execution path.

phases.summary: Decide applicability, flow lens, protected error class, and claim boundary.
Inspect adoption, existing models, templates, state, side effects, and route ownership.
Build or update the minimum faithful Input x State model and representative known-bad path.
Run formal model-first checks, inspect counterexamples, and invoke only triggered owner routes.
Preserve every gap and close only with current model, route, and claim evidence.

quality_floors.summary: Prompt prose, progress, or generated records cannot replace native evidence.
Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, or pass-with-gaps evidence cannot support broad closure.

required_evidence.source: FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact
FlowGuard native command receipt or direct target artifact

route_inventory.summary: Select the owning FlowGuard route and execute the minimum valuable model-first kernel workflow.

do_not_use_when: A direct satellite already owns the boundary or the task is trivial and has no behavior, state, process, or evidence risk.
routes.summary: Select the owning FlowGuard route and execute the minimum valuable model-first kernel workflow.

runtime_lock_policy.applies_when: The model-first-function-flow skill is selected or delegated.
runtime_lock_policy.summary: The target-local contract, route, stage order, checks, evidence, and coverage matrix gate closure.

source_requirements.summary: Missing, stale, skipped, scoped, progress-only, not-run, or pass-with-gaps evidence blocks broad closure.
Executable Input x State modeling and a representative known-bad proof are required before model confidence.
The kernel must defer to a clear public owner and coordinate only unclear, ordinary, or cross-route work.
Accept ordinary modeling, unclear ownership, cross-route coordination, or kernel-owned flowguard_self_maintenance, model_maturation_loop, risk_template_library, risk_evidence_ledger, and cl.
description: FlowGuard kernel for ordinary behavior/state modeling, unclear route selection, cross-route coordination, and kernel-owned internal gates. Use when no direct FlowGuard satellit.
Keep flowguard-plan-detailing-compiler and flowguard-agent-workflow-rehearsal explicit/delegated modes owned by DevelopmentProcessFlow.
Route clear ownership directly to flowguard-existing-model-preflight, flowguard-behavior-commitment-ledger, flowguard-architecture-reduction, flowguard-code-structure-recommendation, flowgu.
Route id: model_first_function_flow; role: kernel; native owner: model_first_function_flow. Decide use_flowguard, skip_with_reason, or needs_human_review through behavior_flow, argument_flo.
The target section 'Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Use when behavior/state/order/retry/side-effect risk needs Input x State -> Set(Output x State) modeling, the correct satellite is unclear, or multiple route outputs must be coordinated.
The target section 'Hard Gates' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Missing, stale, skipped, not_run, progress_only, scoped, or pass_with_gaps evidence cannot support broad done, full-governance, release, archive, or publication claims.
Preserve FlowGuard diagram intent; do not flatten state/process/UI/SourceGuard/TraceGuard/WorldGuard/LogicGuard semantics into a generic flowchart or collapse them without LogicGuard.
Verify the real FlowGuard check engine and AGENTS.md managed record; if import fails, connect the real toolchain or report blocked/partial and never create a fake mini-framework.
The target skill description must be represented in the contract: FlowGuard kernel for ordinary behavior/state modeling, unclear route selection, cross-route coordination, and kernel-owned.
The target skill entrypoint and hard gates must be represented by the runtime contract.
Declared hard gates and required workflow steps must be represented by checks and closure blockers.
Do not force the kernel before a clear peer satellite, use it for trivial copy/format/direct-command work, or treat check-engine helpers as independently triggerable Codex skills.
The target section 'Do Not Use When' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
The target section 'Output Requirements' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Broad model/code/test claims bind obligation ids, owner code contract ids, and current external-contract evidence for the same behavior.
Build/update the smallest faithful finite model with state, side effects, completion evidence, invariants, and a representative known-bad path.
Decide applicability/lens, verify adoption, search current models/templates, and name the protected error class and claim boundary.
Default replacement disposes old fields/wrappers/aliases/alternate success paths; broad behavior needs a current Behavior Commitment Ledger and PPA for path-sensitive rows.
Edit .skillguard/contract-source.json, route registry, or direct references first, then regenerate derived contracts; SkillGuard validates native-integrated ownership and cannot define a pa.
Long-check progress is liveness only; UI/payload claims need real-surface proof; new/deepened models require template harvest closure.
Preserve checked/unchecked/blocked/scoped evidence and close only at the requested current scope.
Read references/route_index.md for the parity-checked route map and references/modeling_protocol.md as the core index; load references/modeling_core_protocol.md, references/modeling_evidenc.
Represent every modeled block as Input x State -> Set(Output x State); do not replace executable modeling with prose or weaken hard invariants to pass.
The target section 'Required Workflow' must be represented by route, check, evidence, or closure coverage.
Return evidence, failures, blockers, skipped_checks, residual_risk, claim_boundary, and typed_next_actions, plus route decision, model snapshot, counterexamples, and current validation stat.
Route finite bad cases, commitments, fields, alignment, meshes, topology, UI, process freshness, conformance, and risk evidence only where triggered.
Run the formal check plan, inspect counterexamples, revise the model/architecture, and record template harvest closure.
Select the smallest owning FlowGuard route and build the minimum valuable executable model for ordinary behavior/state work without becoming a monolith.
